analysis.period_summary: Week delivered $4,517.64 from 158 frontend buyers at $28.59 average revenue per buyer. Email traffic drove 39 tracked conversions and $2,041.80 revenue with no paid spend active. The most critical commercial issue is a severe backend monetization gap: OTO1 combined take rate is only 20.3% (far below the 40% strong benchmark), and OTO2 combined take rate is just 3.8%.

northstar.operating_focus: tracked funnel performance and revenue efficiency
northstar.commentary: Email RPV at $0.40 is directionally healthy for warm traffic, but backend take rates are suppressing average order value significantly.

funnel_leaks.why_it_matters: At 20.3% combined, OTO1 is capturing less than half the strong 40%+ benchmark, leaving significant immediate revenue on the table.
OTO2 at 3.8% is critically weak and represents a near-total monetization failure at the final upsell stage.
Downsell is only capturing 5.1% of frontend buyers, indicating poor downsell trigger logic or offer positioning after OTO1 main rejection.

needle_movers.why: OTO1 combined take rate is 20.3% (24 main at 15.2% + 8 downsell at 5.1%), less than half the 40%+ strong benchmark. This is the single largest backend revenue gap. Backend SKU data confirms 32 total OTO1 sales from 158 frontend buyers.
what: Audit OTO1 main offer page for friction: confirm page load, check copy clarity, validate CTA prominence
Test OTO1 price anchor: evaluate whether $67 main offer needs repositioning or social proof reinforcement
Improve downsell trigger: current 5.1% downsell take suggests poor exit-intent logic or weak downsell offer differentiation
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Directional: lifting OTO1 combined from 20.3% to 35% would add approximately 23 incremental OTO1 sales per week at blended $57 average, worth ~$1,300 additional weekly revenue.

needle_movers.why: OTO2 combined take rate is 3.8% (4 main + 2 downsell from 158 frontend buyers). At below 10%, this represents a near-total failure to monetize the final upsell stage. Backend SKU data confirms only 6 total OTO2 sales.
what: Audit OTO2 presentation timing: confirm it triggers correctly after OTO1 acceptance/rejection
Validate OTO2 offer relevance: ensure Divine Helper positioning aligns with customer intent at this stage
Test OTO2 downsell flow: 2 downsell sales vs 4 main sales suggests downsell may not be triggering for all OTO2 main rejections
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Directional: lifting OTO2 combined from 3.8% to 12% would add approximately 13 incremental OTO2 sales per week at blended $87 average, worth ~$1,130 additional weekly revenue.

needle_movers.why: Campaign #111 (Revamp UI & Copy) drove 3,202 views, 28 conversions, and $1,554.50 revenue at 0.87% CR. This is the highest-volume email campaign and converted nearly 3x the weak 0.3% benchmark. Represents 71.8% of total conversions and 76.1% of total revenue from tracked email campaigns.
what: Protect this campaign structure and creative approach as the clear email winner
Analyze UI & copy changes from #111 vs older email campaigns to identify repeatable winning elements
Test scaled deployment: expand #111-style creative to additional email segments
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Observed: Campaign #111 already delivered $1,554.50 this week. Scaling this approach could replace underperforming email variants and improve overall email RPV.

needle_movers.why: Campaign #51 (Facebook V17 TSL) delivered 263 views, 17 conversions, and $1,332.09 revenue at 6.46% CR. This is an exceptionally strong conversion rate well above the 0.3–0.5% email benchmark, indicating high-intent warm Facebook traffic. No paid spend tracked this week, but historical performance justifies testing controlled paid scale.
what: Test controlled paid scale: allocate modest daily budget to Campaign #51 Facebook traffic source
Monitor paid CPA closely: ensure buyer economics remain below $14.29 target when spend is active
Protect V17 TSL funnel path as demonstrated winner for Facebook traffic
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Cannot quantify without active paid spend data. At 6.46% tracking CR, paid scale is commercially viable if CPA remains under target.

needle_movers.why: destiny_v2 has lower bounce (42.2% vs 58.3%) and higher checkout-from-optin efficiency (21.21% vs 19.73%), but destiny_v1 has higher overall checkout rate (1.36% vs 1.53%) and drove significantly more attributed revenue in variant_sales ($24.28 vs $126.82 for v2, though both are tiny vs 'other' at $2,107.40). Session-based funnel signals favor v2, but attributed sales are too small and attribution too weak to declare a definitive winner.
what: Continue controlled A/B test between destiny_v1 and destiny_v2 with equal traffic allocation
Focus evaluation on sessions-to-checkout efficiency rather than attributed sales until attribution improves
Audit why 'other' captures $2,107.40 in variant_sales while named variants capture minimal attributed revenue
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Mixed signals prevent clean revenue impact estimate. Variant attribution issue must be resolved before projecting lift.

variant_read.label: /destiny (full-screen)
variant_read.why: 17,232 sessions with 58.3% bounce, 6.88% optin rate, and 1.36% checkout rate. Checkout-from-optin is 19.73%, slightly below destiny_v2's 21.21%. Attributed variant_sales shows only $24.28, but the vast majority of sales fall into 'other' ($2,107.40), making attribution unreliable. Session-based signals show functional performance but higher bounce than v2.

variant_read.label: /destiny/v2 (with-headline)
variant_read.why: 917 sessions with lower bounce (42.2% vs destiny_v1's 58.3%), 7.2% optin rate, and 1.53% checkout rate. Checkout-from-optin is 21.21%, slightly better than v1's 19.73%. Attributed variant_sales shows $126.82 vs v1's $24.28, but both are tiny compared to 'other' at $2,107.40. Session-based funnel signals favor v2, but sample size and attribution weakness prevent definitive conclusion.

variant_read.label: /index-v17 (TSL)
variant_read.why: No GA4 session data tracked for index_v17 variant in funnel_variants, but CPV funnel data shows 263 LP views, 18.63% landing-to-optin, 59.18% offer-to-checkout, 58.62% checkout-to-purchase, and 6.46% overall conversion. This TSL path drove 17 tracked conversions and $1,332.09 revenue via Campaign #51 Facebook. Attributed variant_sales shows $776.41 from 17 sales. Strong tracked performance across all funnel stages.

scorecard.status_reason: 2.0% CR, 1 conversion from 50 views, low sample
0.48% CR at high volume, weak but large traffic source
67.1% of frontend sales, healthy premium mix

analysis.data_notes: Attribution weakness: variant_sales captures only $1,361.86 across all named variants vs $2,107.40 in 'other', indicating most sales are not attributed to specific landing variants. CPV conversions (143) are 9.5% below ClickBank frontend sales (158), suggesting some postback loss. Currency note: CPV revenue is SGD converted to USD at 0.74 rate; ClickBank revenue is net vendor USD after fees, explaining the 18.7% revenue差. No paid spend tracked this week, so paid CPA and paid RPV are null. index_v17 has no GA4 session data in funnel_variants but strong CPV funnel data via Campaign #51.
